name: Rhodes Scholarship
eligibility: Citizens of Rhodes constituencies (incl. Bangladesh as of 2018); under 24; bachelor's degree; outstanding intellect, character, leadership.
summary: Bangladesh became a Rhodes constituency in 2018. Selection emphasizes intellectual distinction + service leadership.

name: Chevening Scholarship
eligibility: 2+ years work experience, leadership potential, UK master's offer
summary: UK government scholarship. Bangladesh has a strong Chevening pipeline — leadership essays carry the application.

name: Commonwealth Scholarship
eligibility: Commonwealth citizens, financial need, development impact focus
summary: Strong Bangladesh alumni network. Application weights development relevance of proposed research.

name: DAAD Scholarship
eligibility: Strong academic record, study plan, German or English program
summary: Germany's central scholarship body. Heavy on academic merit and clear research/study motivation.

name: Fulbright Foreign Student Program
eligibility: Bachelor's, leadership, intent to return home for 2 years post-degree
summary: Bangladesh Fulbright commission has annual cohort. Leadership and 'return home' commitment are essential.

name: Knight-Hennessy Scholars (Stanford)
eligibility: Admission to any Stanford grad program, leadership, civic mindset, under-32 ideally
summary: Adds full funding to any Stanford grad program. Independent app from program admission.

name: Schwarzman Scholars
eligibility: Bachelor's, under 29, English proficient, leadership in any field
summary: One-year Tsinghua master's in Global Affairs. Highly selective leadership scholarship.

name: Gates Cambridge Scholarship
value: Full tuition + stipend + travel + family allowance
eligibility: Non-UK citizen, Cambridge grad admission, academic excellence, leadership, commitment to others
summary: Full funding on top of Cambridge grad admission. Very selective; intellectual ability + commitment to improving lives of others.

name: NUS ASEAN Undergraduate Scholarship
eligibility: ASEAN citizen (incl. Bangladesh-equivalent SAARC programs), strong academics
summary: NUS offers undergrad scholarships for South + Southeast Asian students with strong academics.

name: Polish Government Scholarship
eligibility: Developing country citizen, Polish or English program admission
summary: Low-competition pathway to EU education for Bangladeshi students with strong academics.

name: Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship
eligibility: Commonwealth and developing country citizens; 2+ years work experience preferred
summary: Strong development focus. Applicants from Bangladesh need clear post-study impact plan.

name: Australia Awards Scholarships
eligibility: Developing country citizen, 2+ years work experience, development link
summary: Bangladesh is a focus country. Application weights development relevance.

name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master's
eligibility: Bachelor's, strong academics, English/EU lang proficiency
summary: Two-country EU master's with full funding. Highly competitive — research fit and motivation letter matter most.

name: Korean Government Scholarship (GKS)
value: Tuition + monthly stipend + Korean language training
summary: Korea's flagship scholarship. Includes a year of Korean language training before degree.

name: Swedish Institute Scholarships for Global Professionals
eligibility: Strong leadership in development fields, 3000+ hr work/volunteer
summary: Selective Swedish scholarship for leaders in development. Bangladesh is an eligible country.

name: KAIST International Undergraduate Scholarship
summary: Direct STEM merit scholarship at one of Asia's top tech universities. Olympiad results help.

name: Yale Need-Based Aid (International)
eligibility: Yale admission + financial need (need-blind for internationals)
summary: Yale meets 100% of demonstrated need for internationals. No separate application needed.

name: MIT Need-Based Aid (International)
eligibility: MIT admission + financial need (need-blind for internationals)
summary: MIT is need-blind for international applicants — financial need does not affect admission decision.
